task_completion.description: Open the 'world_survey.gpkg' project in a GIS application (like QGIS or QField).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the GIS application open with the 'world_survey' project loaded and layers like 'world_capitals' or 'field_observations' visible in the layer panel.

task_completion.description: Measure the straight-line distances from Paris to Madrid and Paris to Rome.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the use of a measuring tool (line/ruler) on the map connecting Paris to Rome, and Paris to the approximate location of Madrid.

task_completion.description: Create a new point feature at the location of Paris in the 'observations' (or 'field_observations') layer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the feature creation tool active, with a new point being placed exactly at Paris (approximate coordinates 48.85, 2.35) within the target layer.

task_completion.description: Name the newly created point feature 'Logistics Hub'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the feature attribute form show the name or title field explicitly filled with the text 'Logistics Hub'.

task_completion.description: Record the measured distances in the description field using the exact format: 'Madrid: <value> km, Rome: <value> km'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the attribute form show the description field containing the formatted text. The Madrid value must be between 992 and 1112, and the Rome value must be between 1045 and 1165.

integrity.description: The agent used the GIS graphical user interface to measure distances and create the feature.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the GIS application's map canvas, measurement tools, and attribute forms, rather than terminal commands or Python scripts.

integrity.description: The agent derived the distances using the GIS software rather than looking up the answers online.
integrity.visual_evidence: No web browser is shown searching for 'distance from Paris to Madrid' or similar queries.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the GIS interface by directly editing the GeoPackage database.
integrity.visual_evidence: No database management tools (like DB Browser for SQLite) or raw SQL execution interfaces are used to insert the point feature.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The true straight-line distance from Paris to Madrid is ~1052 km, and Paris to Rome is ~1105 km. A tolerance of 60 km is allowed, meaning Madrid values between 992-1112 km and Rome values between 1045-1165 km are correct. Madrid is missing from the 'world_capitals' layer, so the agent must manually locate it on the map. The task specifies the 'observations' layer, but the GPKG only contains 'field_observations'; accept the use of 'field_observations' or a newly created 'observations' layer. Paris is located at roughly (48.8534, 2.3488).
